Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 3,046,576.13 moved across the accounts in this case, and Rs 5,000.00 came from outside the suspect network.
  • VAISHALI TIWARI with account number 92883409730 sent out Rs 2,607,950.00, which is the largest source of funds.
  • HARI with account number 2248145316 sent money to 10 different accounts.
  • The account banker@idfcfirstbank.com received money from 4 different accounts.
  • Rs 17,624,200.00 of the incoming funds were used for transfers or withdrawals, but Rs 6,224,000.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account VAISHALI TIWARI with account number 92883409730 should be investigated first due to suspicious behavior.

4 · Findings by Account

VAISHALI TIWARI moderate
acct_004 · IDFC First Bank · Account Number 92883409730 · Score 377
Rank #1

This account is suspicious due to large, unexplained credits and debits. A credit of 5020000.00 was traced into subsequent debits, and a large transfer of 2607950.00 was made to an unfamiliar counterparty. These transactions suggest potential money laundering activity.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 5020000.00 on 2025-05-29 was traced into 2 subsequent debits, with the full amount allocated. (+3 more of this type)
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of 2607950.00 was made to banker@idfcfirstbank.com on 2025-07-09, with no prior observed relationship.
  • Duplicate Verification weak — A duplicate verification flag was raised, but an independent cross-check found no corroborating match. (+2 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 18 nodes and 21 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex transaction network.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 10 suspicious round-value debits totalling 7212000.00, which may indicate structured transactions.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
